title: Sales Opportunity Brief — Revive Bond-Repair Shampoo & Conditioner System

sections.heading: Client Overview
sections.content:
Brand: Revive
Segment: Salon Professional
Product Concept: Bond-Repair Shampoo + Conditioner System (2-SKU launch)
Target Consumer: Salon clients and at-home users seeking professional-grade bond-repair haircare
Market Positioning: Prestige mass market

sections.heading: Product & Pricing Summary
sections.content:
SKU 1: Bond-Repair Shampoo — Retail Price: $28.00 USD
SKU 2: Bond-Repair Conditioner — Retail Price: $32.00 USD
Total System Retail Value: $60.00 USD
Channel: Salon professional retail and direct-to-consumer (at-home use)
Packaging Format: [To be confirmed]
Fill Size: [To be confirmed]

sections.heading: Target Claims
sections.content:
The following cosmetic claims have been identified for this system:
• Bond repair
• Breakage reduction
• Frizz control

Claim Type: Cosmetic claims only — no drug claims are being pursued at this stage.
Claim Substantiation Strategy: [To be confirmed — recommend discussing clinical or consumer perception testing options]

sections.heading: Volume Forecast
sections.content:
Launch Volume (Phase 1): 5,000 units per SKU (10,000 units total)
Scale Target (18 months): 25,000 units per SKU (50,000 units total)
Growth Multiple: 5x over 18-month horizon
Forecast Confidence: Initial projection provided by client — detailed ramp schedule [To be confirmed]

sections.heading: Geographic Markets
sections.content:
• United States (primary launch market)
• Canada (simultaneous launch)

Note: Bilingual labeling (English/French) will be required for Canadian market compliance. Regulatory requirements differ between US and Canada — see regulatory section below.

sections.heading: Key Sales Opportunity Highlights
sections.content:
• Two-SKU system sale with strong system attachment potential (consumers purchasing both products together)
• Prestige mass price point supports healthy margin structure
• Bond-repair is a high-growth, trend-driven category with proven consumer demand (reference: Olaplex effect on the market)
• Salon professional channel provides credibility and word-of-mouth amplification
• Clear 5x volume scale trajectory represents significant revenue growth opportunity
• No drug claims simplifies regulatory pathway and speeds time to market

title: Client Proposal — Revive Bond-Repair Haircare System

sections.heading: Executive Summary
sections.content: CosmoLab is pleased to present this proposal to Revive for the development and supply of a professional-grade Bond-Repair Shampoo and Conditioner system. This proposal outlines our recommended development approach, production scope, and partnership structure to bring Revive's vision to market across the US and Canada.

sections.heading: Proposed Scope of Work
sections.content:
Phase 1 — Formulation Development
• Bond-Repair Shampoo: Development of a sulfate-[to be confirmed] shampoo formula incorporating bond-repair actives supporting breakage reduction and frizz control claims
• Bond-Repair Conditioner: Development of a complementary conditioning formula with bond-repair technology for cohesive system performance
• Fragrance alignment across both SKUs for brand cohesion [Fragrance brief: To be confirmed]
• Formula optimization for salon professional consumer expectations (texture, lather, rinse, sensory)

Phase 2 — Claim Substantiation
• Recommended: Consumer perception study and/or in-vitro breakage/bond testing to substantiate bond repair, breakage reduction, and frizz control claims
• Testing scope and vendor: [To be confirmed]

Phase 3 — Regulatory & Labeling
• US FDA cosmetic labeling compliance (MoCRA considerations)
• Canada Health Canada cosmetic notification and bilingual label requirements
• INCI ingredient listing finalization

Phase 4 — Production
• Phase 1 production run: 5,000 units per SKU
• Scale production planning for 25,000 units per SKU within 18 months

sections.heading: Pricing Estimate
sections.content:
Formulation Development Fee (2 SKUs): [To be confirmed — pending brief and complexity assessment]
Sample and Stability Testing: [To be confirmed]
Claim Substantiation Testing: [To be confirmed]
Unit Cost of Goods (Phase 1 — 5,000 units/SKU): [To be confirmed — MOQ and BOM pending]
Unit Cost of Goods (Scale — 25,000 units/SKU): [To be confirmed — volume pricing to apply]

Note: Detailed cost of goods will be provided upon receipt of packaging specifications, fill size confirmation, and formula brief sign-off.

sections.heading: Indicative Project Timeline
sections.content:
Week 1–2: Project kick-off, brief alignment, fragrance selection initiation
Week 2–8: Formulation development and internal benchmarking
Week 8–10: Sample submission to client for review
Week 10–14: Client feedback, formula iterations, and approval
Week 14–20: Stability and compatibility testing
Week 16–20: Claim substantiation testing (parallel track)
Week 18–22: Regulatory review and label finalization
Week 22–26: Production of Phase 1 run (5,000 units/SKU)

Full timeline subject to confirmation pending project complexity and client response times.

sections.heading: Why CosmoLab
sections.content:
• Deep expertise in bond-repair and protein-based haircare technology
• Experience serving salon professional brands at prestige mass price positioning
• Dual US/Canada regulatory capability — streamlined path to both markets
• Scalable manufacturing to support Revive's 5x growth trajectory
• End-to-end support from formulation through production and compliance

sections.heading: Next Steps
sections.content:
To advance this opportunity, we recommend the following:
1. Schedule a technical kick-off call to align on formula brief, actives preferences, and fragrance direction
2. Confirm packaging format and fill sizes to enable accurate COG modeling
3. Align on claim substantiation testing approach and budget
4. Execute development agreement / project initiation paperwork

title: Competitive & Market Positioning Summary — Bond-Repair Haircare Category

sections.heading: Category Overview
sections.content: The bond-repair haircare category has experienced significant growth driven by consumer awareness of hair damage at the chemical and structural level. Pioneered at mass scale by brands such as Olaplex, the category has expanded across all price tiers. The prestige mass segment — where Revive is positioning at $28/$32 per SKU — represents a high-value sweet spot between accessible and aspirational.

sections.heading: Competitive Landscape (Illustrative)
sections.content:
Key competitors in the bond-repair shampoo and conditioner space include:
• Olaplex No. 4 & No. 5 — Retail ~$30/$30, salon-originated, strong bond-repair equity, widely distributed
• K18 Peptide Prep Shampoo — Retail ~$38, biotech-forward positioning
• Redken Acidic Bonding Concentrate — Retail ~$28/$28, professional brand, broad salon distribution
• Briogeo Don't Despair, Repair! — Retail ~$42/$42, clean positioning, prestige natural
• Amika The Kure Bond Repair Shampoo/Conditioner — Retail ~$26/$28, trend-forward, salon channel

Revive's $28/$32 price points are well-positioned within the competitive set, with opportunity to differentiate on formulation technology, salon professional credibility, and brand story.

Note: Competitor pricing and positioning subject to change. Sales team to verify current market data prior to client presentations.

sections.heading: Revive Differentiation Opportunities
sections.content:
• Salon-exclusive or salon-first launch strategy to build professional credibility before broader distribution
• System-selling approach (shampoo + conditioner purchased as a pair) to increase basket size and consumer commitment
• Bond-repair + frizz control dual-benefit positioning addresses multiple consumer pain points simultaneously
• Prestige mass price point accessible to a broader consumer base than ultra-premium competitors
• Potential for salon stylist advocacy and recommendation as a key acquisition channel

sections.heading: Consumer Insight Summary
sections.content:
Target Consumer Profile:
• Salon clients who receive chemical treatments (color, relaxers, keratin, highlights) — high bond-damage concern
• At-home users seeking professional-grade results without salon visits
• Consumers who are ingredient and efficacy-aware — will respond to substantiated claims and visible results

Key Consumer Pain Points Addressed:
• Breakage and weakness from chemical or heat damage
• Frizz and lack of smoothness
• Distrust of generic shampoos — seeking targeted, professional solutions

Purchase Motivators:
• Professional recommendation (salon stylist endorsement)
• Visible, fast results
• System/ritual concept — perceived efficacy of using matched products

title: Regulatory Snapshot — US & Canada Cosmetic Compliance (Haircare)

sections.heading: Overview
sections.content: This document provides a high-level regulatory overview for the Revive Bond-Repair Shampoo and Conditioner system intended for sale in the United States and Canada. All claims confirmed as cosmetic claims only — no drug claims are being pursued.

sections.heading: United States — FDA / MoCRA Requirements
sections.content:
Applicable Regulation: FDA Modernization of Cosmetics Regulation Act (MoCRA), signed into law December 2022

Key Requirements for US Market:
• Facility Registration: Cosmetic manufacturing and processing facilities must be registered with FDA. Registration renewal required every two years.
• Product Listing: Each cosmetic product (each SKU) must be listed with FDA, including product category, ingredients (INCI), and responsible person information.
• Labeling Requirements:
  — Product identity statement
  — Net quantity of contents
  — Name and address of responsible person
  — INCI ingredient list (descending order of predominance)
  — Any required warnings
• Safety Substantiation: Responsible person must maintain records substantiating product safety.
• Serious Adverse Event Reporting: Mandatory reporting of serious adverse events to FDA within 15 business days.
• No drug claims permitted — bond repair, breakage reduction, and frizz control are acceptable cosmetic claims if not structured as drug claims.

MoCRA Compliance Deadlines: [To be confirmed with regulatory affairs team — phase-in timelines apply based on company size]

sections.heading: Canada — Health Canada Requirements
sections.content:
Applicable Regulation: Cosmetic Regulations under the Canada Consumer Product Safety Act (CCPSA)

Key Requirements for Canadian Market:
• Cosmetic Notification: The responsible party (importer or manufacturer) must notify Health Canada within 10 days of first sale in Canada. Notification includes product name, manufacturer/importer details, and ingredient list.
• Bilingual Labeling: All consumer-facing labels must include English and French text (bilingual requirement under Consumer Packaging and Labelling Act).
• INCI Ingredient List: Required on label in both English and French (INCI names are generally language-neutral but label context must be bilingual).
• Hotlist Compliance: All formula ingredients must be reviewed against Health Canada's Cosmetic Ingredient Hotlist (restricted and prohibited substances).
• Safety: Products must be safe for use as intended; Safety Data is the responsibility of the notifier.
• No NPN (Natural Product Number) required for standard cosmetic claims — bond repair, breakage reduction, and frizz control are cosmetic claims and do not trigger drug/NHP classification.

Note: Ensure French translations of all label copy, marketing claims, and directions for use are reviewed by a qualified French-language specialist.

sections.heading: Claim Classification Guidance
sections.content:
The following claims have been reviewed at a high level for classification:

• 'Bond repair' — Cosmetic claim ✓ (refers to improving appearance/condition of hair structure; does not imply physiological drug action if positioned correctly)
• 'Breakage reduction' — Cosmetic claim ✓ (structural/mechanical benefit; acceptable as cosmetic with substantiation)
• 'Frizz control' — Cosmetic claim ✓ (well-established cosmetic claim category)

Claims to Avoid (Drug Claim Triggers):
• References to treating, curing, or preventing a disease or condition
• Claims implying action on scalp biology or follicle physiology in a therapeutic context
• 'Restores bonds at the molecular level' — use with caution; may require legal/regulatory review depending on framing

Recommendation: All final claim language should be reviewed by regulatory affairs and legal counsel prior to use on packaging or marketing materials.

sections.heading: Recommended Next Steps — Regulatory
sections.content:
1. Initiate FDA facility registration and product listing process under MoCRA
2. Prepare Health Canada Cosmetic Notification for both SKUs
3. Conduct Hotlist review of final formula INCI lists
4. Commission bilingual label copy translation and review
5. Establish safety substantiation file for both SKUs
6. Confirm claim language with regulatory and legal review
